---
title: "conversation starters for support"
format: html
---

## Conversation Starters for Support

Isolation increases relapse risk. Short, low-pressure conversations can rebuild connection and make support easier to access.

You do not need to perform perfectly. The goal is to open contact and stay engaged.

Interpersonal stress is a common relapse risk. Communication habits, emotional reactivity, and body language all affect how quickly conflict escalates and how supported you feel afterward.

When communication becomes clearer and more regulated, stress drops and problem-solving improves. These skills protect recovery by reducing isolation, resentment, and impulsive reactions during difficult interactions.

::: callout
### Instructions
Practice simple openers you can use this week.
:::

| Context | Starter line | Follow-up question |
|---|---|---|
| Recovery meeting | | |
| Work or school | | |
| Family setting | | |
| New social setting | | |
